title: MICCAI STSR 2026 Pre-Task: 2D Images Segmentation
terms:
# 📃 Terms

To ensure a fair and transparent competition, participants must accept the rules if they wish to participate in the STSR 2026 Challenge:

- All participants should register for this challenge with their real names, affiliations (including department, full name of institute/company, country), and affiliation E-mails. Incomplete and redundant registrations will be removed without notice. Each team can have at most six people.

- All participants must submit a complete solution to this challenge during the validation and testing phase. A complete solution includes a complete source code and a qualified methodology paper (at least 8 pages, LNCS format).

- All participants should agree that the submitted short papers can be publicly available to the community on the challenge website, and organizers can use the information provided by the participants, including scores, predicted labels, and papers. If registered participants do not make submissions (source code and paper), their results will not be considered.

- Participants can only register one team and account. Participants from the same research group are also not allowed to register multiple teams. STSR 2026 Organizers keep the right to disqualify such participants.

- Redistribution or transfer of data or data link is not allowed. Participants should use the data only by themselves. The challenge data will be free to use after the submission of a challenge summary paper, but commercial use is prohibited.

- Participants should develop fully automatic methods based on the training set only and any manual interventions (e.g., manually annotate the segmentation mask) are not allowed. For a fair comparison, participants are not allowed to use any additional data.

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
## MICCAI STSR 2026 Pre-Task 2D Images Segmentation This task is a prerequisite for STS26. To prevent dataset misuse, upon completion, you will be eligible to access the STS26 main competition dataset. Please submit the model's inference results('data/test/masks.zip') on the 'data/test/imgs/' data to the platform as a proof.(just takes about 1 hour) If there's any question, please contact directly at SemiTeethSegChallenge@outlook.com ## 📖 Project Overview 👌A simple 2D image segmentation task using [U-Net](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505). - **Step1: Download the Pre-Task data and training modal**
To avoid the misuse of our data, you may just finish this simple (very simple) pre-task and fill out the Data Access Agreement at first to get access to the data. Data site:
-[Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/datasets/Ricoooo/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task)
-[Modelscope](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/lizhii/MICCAI-STS26-Challenge-Pre-Task/summary)
-[Baidu Netdisk(extracting code = bm2u)](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1U090bZnMGEJQaD3jwqaQuA)
-[Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1lER9eIavr99g28aTO0kuxIcos_k9FBSx?usp=sharing)
Or you could use the [STS-Skill](https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill) as when you use Claude Code, like:
"Use STS-Skill https://github.com/ricoleehduu/sts-skill to help me finishing the Pre-Task." - **Step2:** Submit result to the online submission system. - **Step3:** Fill out the registration form and submit the agreements to WPS Form or Google Form:
- [For domestic participants (WPS Form)](https://f.wps.cn/g/VV1UmM0O/)
- [For international participants (Google Form)](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf4hxOwxQdkIHkSICzlD2xnlAuPWr3EvUeB7oeuRKRPUimAKQ/viewform?usp=publish-editor) ## 💻 Data Structure ``` data/ ├── imgs/ # Training images ├── masks/ # Training masks └── test/ ├── imgs/ # Test images (50 samples) └── masks/ # Where you put your references (50 samples) ``` ## ☑️ Quick Start Code is available at [U-Net](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505) ### Environment Requirements - Python 3.6+ - PyTorch 1.13+ - CUDA (recommended) ### Install Dependencies ```bash pip install -r requirements.txt ``` ### Train Model ```bash python train.py --amp ``` ## 🛠️ Usage ### Training Parameters ```console > python train.py -h usage: train.py [-h] [--epochs E] [--batch-size B] [--learning-rate LR] [--load LOAD] [--scale SCALE] [--validation VAL] [--amp] Train the UNet on images and target masks optional arguments: -h, --help show this help message and exit --epochs E, -e E Number of epochs --batch-size B, -b B Batch size --learning-rate LR, -l LR Learning rate --load LOAD, -f LOAD Load model from a .pth file --scale SCALE, -s SCALE Downscaling factor of the images --validation VAL, -v VAL Percent of the data that is used as validation (0-100) --amp Use mixed precision ``` ### Prediction #### Batch Processing ```bash python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs -o data/test/masks python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i data/test/imgs -o data/test/masks -t 0.3 ``` #### Single File ```bash python predict.py -m checkpoints/checkpoint_epoch1.pth -i input.png -o output.png ``` #### Prediction Parameters ```console > python predict.py -h usage: predict.py [-h] --model FILE --input INPUT [INPUT ...] [--output INPUT [INPUT ...]] [--viz] [--no-save] [--mask-threshold MASK_THRESHOLD] [--scale SCALE] [--bilinear] [--classes CLASSES] Predict masks from input images optional arguments: -h, --help show this help message and exit --model FILE, -m FILE Model checkpoint path --input INPUT, -i INPUT Input image(s) or directory --output INPUT, -o INPUT Output image(s) or directory --viz, -v Visualize results --no-save, -n Do not save output masks --mask-threshold, -t Threshold for mask binarization (default: 0.5) --scale, -s Image scale factor (default: 0.5) --bilinear Use bilinear upsampling --classes, -c Number of classes (default: 1) ``` ## ‼️ Submission Format Please make sure there's no extra folder in your ZIP file and the size of the image you predict need to be the same as the original image!! Please keep the pixel value in the range of (0, 255) ``` ─mask.zip ├──A-150.png ├──... └──C-198.png ``` ## ✅ Reference Original paper by Olaf Ronneberger, Philipp Fischer, Thomas Brox: [U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation](https://arxiv.org/abs/1505.04597) Original code by milesial: [Pytorch-UNet](https://github.com/milesial/Pytorch-UNet?tab=readme-ov-file#with-docker)
